summary: Jeff Atwood announces his appearance on the .NET Rocks podcast, a long-running developer-focused internet radio show dating back to 2002. He expresses gratitude for the opportunity and notes the show's history of interviewing notable developers like Steve McConnell. The 64-minute interview covers themes from his Coding Horror blog. Atwood humbly positions himself among the show's impressive roster of past guests, including his own company's CEO Scott Stanfield.
key_points: Atwood was interviewed on .NET Rocks episode 232, a podcast he had listened to for years
The show has a notable history going back to 2002 with interviews of influential developers like Steve McConnell
The interview runs 64 minutes and covers topics from the Coding Horror blog
Atwood demonstrates characteristic humility by noting others interviewed were 'far more interesting' than himself
money_quotes: .NET Rocks! is a long running internet radio talk show for software developers that goes all the way back to 2002.
They've interviewed some very notable software developers along the way including Steve McConnell, and many other people far more interesting than myself.
It was my great honor to participate in this week's episode of .NET Rocks!
key_insight: Even established bloggers value participating in the broader developer community through podcasts and shared conversations.
